content:
100% agree. I'm assuming they're trying to draw in the casual gacha gamer crowd who wants easy mode story, but the reason Morimens has such a dedicated fanbase is **because **of the difficulty of the game. Nerfing the difficulty isn't going to get the casual crowd to stick with the game, because they will find any myriad of reasons to leave. You're alienating your core playerbase in your pursuit of a playerbase that doesn't care about the game.

Lowering the normal story difficulty is fine, because the difficulty ramp up after chapter 6 is significant and many people are unprepared for it, but what you did was **way too much**. The game is now a complete joke, you might as well remove the gameplay for story mode with how easy it is now. Not only that, but nerfing hard mode is an idiotic choice and should be reverted immediately. **The purpose of hard mode is to provide a challenge**, which it doesn't anymore now that the difficulty has been nerfed down to 1/3rd of what it was before, which is **absolutely insane**. Story mode difficulty should have been lowered **at most** by 20-30%, but the 60-70% nerf you did is **utterly ridiculous**.

I am part of a small social group of gamers, and I have brought in 7-8 players who are all dedicated fans right now, and **every single one of them** is dedicated **because **the game is a difficult game that provides a real challenge and actually requires them to learn how to play. If this was just another one of the 1000 other boring, no difficulty gacha games on the market that people only play to autoplay through the story, **not a single one of them** would still be playing. All of us have tried out the common games on the market (ZZZ, HSR, etc), but ended up quitting because the games were too easy and didn't keep our interest. I advise you strongly to rethink your decision because your choice will do more harm than good. <@633215996508766229> <@1131790839291596841> <@&1166257943742787615>

new normal -> story mode
old normal -> normal
new hard -> hard mode
old hard -> new diff insanity
madness -> madness

💯💯💯💯💯 

Normal mode def shouldn't be lowered more than 20% from old one and even then only like last 2-3 chs maybe from arc1 as their difficulty suddenly skyrocketed. Rest I wouldn't even touch and leave it as it was. 

And same here. Me and some of my friends lit play it cuz it's difficulty, challenge that it brings. And actual need to think about ur moves or ull get roasted, not like in other useless auto battlers where u lit just watch bot do all - boring. And without that difficulty charm of game is lost. Like new ppl won't even have that thrilling moments when u lit survive with like 5% dr left etc... 

So bring back old normal (can be lowered a lil in last chs as I already said but mostly should stay as it was) and hard mode!

I hate to do this. Because it goes against my own morals as a human being, not being bothersome...

But.... Bump.

Just wanted to chime in that the reason I fell in love with the game, and then proceeded to spend on said game, was because it challenged me. I'd have been less inclined to pull for characters if I could just face roll through content... in fact, I probably would've dropped it like I did Reverse 1999 for being too simple and lacking in difficulty.

Also, while this is very much a low sample size, I know at least one person whose main reason for dropping the game one day after trying it out... was because it's so trivially easy that they felt it didn't matter how well they played.

They even complained that they didn't feel any stakes in the story just because they felt like they were playing as "invincible monster hunters" so there was no tension or feeling of fighting against overwhelming odds.

I also feel like the disparity between Arc 1 and the difficult content now is so stark that when the new players start dipping their toes into Phantasmal Dive and especially D-Effect Zone, the whiplash is going to be huge.
Bare minimum, I think Hard Mode should be reverted to what it was. Let new players at least have a taste of what the game's difficulty can be like, on what amounts to an optional version of Arc 1.

I feel like pseychie had put it well into words in the "hardest gacha game" video.

Morimens feels unfair, but what keeps you going, is the fact that you can do some unfair things to it.
Hell I'd take hard mode if chapter 8-9 was even close to normal mode boss hp. 120k.

I think it's somewhat ironic that Pseychie's videos, which is objectively one of (if not the) biggest boosts to Morimens english player count, nonstop promote Morimens as the hardest gacha he's ever played
just for them to turn around and make it another meaningless autoplay game with no difficulty like any of the other million on the market
that is certainly a choice to make

I have nothing to say that hasn’t already been said…

it’s fine if you wanna reduce the difficulty a little bit, but what we got is pretty excesive

content:
Hi, I installed the game before the patch and had difficulties finishing the in-game download; removed, cleaned, redownloaded, and downloaded the content in-game but nothing seemed to work. I was advised in another discord to wait until after the patch and see if it helped. Now the download did finish, and the game starts to the main menu, but if I click on "Enter Mythag" nothing happens but an "x" appears at the top right of the screen. It looks like some window is invisible but blocking the screen. If I click the "x" button I get the warning that connection to the silver server is not available or something. 

System: Linux Mint, using Steam, Proton version 9 (newer versions do not allow the game to start)
Tried: remove/reinstall/redownload, different proton versions, verify game files, steam overlay on and off, restarting steam/PC/Router, using a VPN to change connection to Germany and Hong Kong. 
Using official launcher: If I install the launcher directly from the website, using Wine with ProtonTricks, it also gets to the main menu and displays the same bug.
Using iPhone: It works on iPhone, a login screen pops up immediately when I get to the main menu, but I don't want to play the game on iPhone. 

I do not have an account (did not get to that part yet) other than the Steam account. 
No antivirus is blocking access, and access through router is open to Steam and steam games.
Installed size: 9,7 gb
Build ID: 20733169

Does anyone have any suggestions on what I can try to fix this?
thread_title: ✅「Answered」Cannot click on main menu / invisible screen with X button top right
